chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH AT KARACHI Criminal Bail Application No.2840 of 2024 DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATUREs OF JUDGEs For hearing of bail application -------------------------------------------- 24.02.2025 M/s Uzair A.K. Ghouri & Ahtasham-ul-Hasan Narejo, advocates for applicant Ms. Seema Zaidi, Additional Prosecutor General Sindh ------------------------------------------- Applicant/accused Akhtar Ali son of Haji Naseeruddin, seeks post arrest bail in FIR No.224/2024, registered at P.S. Baghdadi for offence under sections 23 (a), 24, 25, 32 Sindh Arms Act, 2013. It is alleged in the FIR that he possess a licensed pistol, which was used by his brother in offence under section 302, PPC, arising out of FIR No.220/2024 of PS Baghdadi. It is not alleged in FIR that applicant has used the weapon in the commission of offence but the only allegation against him is that his brother has used the same. Counsel for applicant/accused submits that the applicant is in custody since 28.08.2024; case has been challaned and applicant is no more required for further investigation. Learned Additional Prosecutor General Sindh submits that applicant/accused is not involved in any other case, except the present case. Having heard the learned counsel for parties and perused the material available on record. Prima facie, it appears that sufficient grounds are available to make out a case of further inquiry. As such, applicant/accused Akhtar Ali son of Haji Naseeruddin is admitted to post arrest bail in the foresaid FIR, subject to his furnishing solvent surety in the sum of Rs.100,000/- (Rupees one lac) and P.R. Bond in the like amount to the satisfaction of trial Court.

As such, applicant/accused Akhtar Ali son of Haji Naseeruddin is admitted to post arrest bail in the foresaid FIR, subject to his furnishing solvent surety in the sum of Rs.100,000/- (Rupees one lac) and P.R. Bond in the like amount to the satisfaction of trial Court. However, it is made clear that the above observations are tentative in nature, the same would not be prejudice the case of either party at trial. The instant criminal bail application is disposed of in the above terms. J U D G E Gulsher/PS
